name: campus-animal-ecosystem
description: Multiverse Campus animal ecosystem: species profiles with behavioral traits, habitat biomes, social structure, breeding rules, and lore.

triples.subject: Rabbit
triples.object: Quick and cautious, rabbits dart between meadow patches at dawn and dusk. They thump their hind legs to warn the group when danger approaches.

triples.subject: Rabbit
triples.object: breeds in spring, summer, 30-day gestation, 3-7 offspring, polygamous

triples.subject: Squirrel
triples.object: Endlessly energetic, squirrels dash between trees, burying acorns they\

triples.subject: Squirrel
triples.object: breeds in spring, 44-day gestation, 2-5 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Sparrow
triples.object: Cheerful little birds that hop along paths and rooftops, always looking for crumbs. Where there\

triples.subject: Sparrow
triples.object: flyer, forager, herding, shy, arboreal, diurnal

triples.subject: Sparrow
triples.object: breeds in spring, summer, 14-day gestation, 3-5 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Robin
triples.object: Bold little songbirds with russet breasts who hop across lawns cocking their heads to listen for worms. Robins are among the first birds to sing at dawn and the last to stop at dusk, filling the campu

triples.subject: Robin
triples.object: breeds in spring, summer, 14-day gestation, 3-5 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Robin
triples.object: singing from the highest perch at sunrise

triples.subject: Finch
triples.object: Tiny, colorful seed-crackers that travel in chattering flocks and descend on garden flowers like a living cloud. Their rapid twittering is the background music of campus life.

triples.subject: Finch
triples.object: flyer, forager, herding, skittish, arboreal, diurnal

triples.subject: Finch
triples.object: breeds in spring, summer, 13-day gestation, 3-6 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Finch
triples.object: elaborate song duets from adjacent branches

triples.subject: Starling
triples.object: Iridescent, noisy, and impossibly social, starlings form murmurations at dusk — swirling clouds of hundreds that wheel and pivot as one mind. They mimic sounds they hear: door creaks, phone ringtones,

triples.subject: Starling
triples.object: breeds in spring, 12-day gestation, 4-6 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Starling
triples.object: fluffing iridescent feathers and singing a medley of mimicked sounds

triples.subject: Frog
triples.object: Patient amphibians that sit motionless for hours, then leap with startling speed when a bug passes. They sing in chorus after rain.

triples.subject: Frog
triples.object: breeds in spring, 90-day gestation, 4-10 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Frog
triples.object: chorus singing at the pond edge

triples.subject: Butterfly
triples.object: Drifting on invisible currents, butterflies trace lazy spirals between flowers. Their flight paths look random but always end where the nectar is.

triples.subject: Butterfly
triples.object: breeds in spring, summer, 30-day gestation, 2-6 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Red Fox
triples.object: Clever and cautious, foxes trot through the campus at twilight. They watch humans with intelligent eyes but vanish the moment you reach for your phone.

triples.subject: Red Fox
triples.object: breeds in winter, 52-day gestation, 3-6 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Red Fox
triples.object: the pair hunts together during courtship

triples.subject: Deer
triples.object: Graceful and wary, deer move through the forest edges in small family groups. They freeze when startled, ears swiveling like satellite dishes, before bounding away in elegant leaps.

triples.subject: Deer
triples.object: breeds in autumn, 200-day gestation, 1-2 offspring, polygamous

triples.subject: Deer
triples.object: bucks spar with antlers, does watch from a distance

triples.subject: Raccoon
triples.object: Masked bandits of the campus, raccoons investigate everything with their dexterous paws. They\

triples.subject: Raccoon
triples.object: breeds in winter, spring, 63-day gestation, 2-5 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Raccoon
triples.object: males bring shiny objects to females

triples.subject: Blue Heron
triples.object: Standing perfectly still at the water\

triples.subject: Blue Heron
triples.object: breeds in spring, 28-day gestation, 2-4 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Blue Heron
triples.object: elaborate aerial displays and bill clacking

triples.subject: Pond Turtle
triples.object: Unhurried and unbothered, turtles bask on warm rocks and slip into the water at the first sign of trouble. They carry their homes wherever they go.

triples.subject: Pond Turtle
triples.object: breeds in spring, summer, 75-day gestation, 2-8 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Pond Turtle
triples.object: males bob their heads and follow females persistently

triples.subject: Great Horned Owl
triples.object: Silent hunters of the night, owls perch in high places and scan the ground with enormous eyes. Their heads rotate with an eerie deliberateness.

triples.subject: Great Horned Owl
triples.object: percher, predator, solitary, territorial, arboreal, nocturnal

triples.subject: Great Horned Owl
triples.object: breeds in winter, spring, 33-day gestation, 1-3 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Grey Wolf
triples.object: Pack hunters with complex social bonds, wolves communicate through howls, body posture, and shared hunts. They range far but always return to the den.

triples.subject: Grey Wolf
triples.object: breeds in winter, 63-day gestation, 3-6 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Grey Wolf
triples.object: the alpha pair howls together under the moon

triples.subject: Village Cat
triples.object: Independent and aloof, village cats claim sunny spots on warm surfaces as their personal territory. They tolerate human presence with regal indifference.

triples.subject: Village Cat
triples.object: breeds in spring, summer, autumn, 63-day gestation, 2-5 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Village Dog
triples.object: Friendly and loyal, village dogs wander between buildings, greeting everyone with a wagging tail. They nap in doorways and bark at nothing in particular.

triples.subject: Village Dog
triples.object: breeds in spring, autumn, 63-day gestation, 3-6 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Golden Retriever
triples.object: The friendliest face on campus. Golden retrievers greet everyone like a long-lost best friend and will carry anything in their mouth just to be helpful.

triples.subject: Golden Retriever
triples.object: breeds in spring, autumn, 63-day gestation, 4-8 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Golden Retriever
triples.object: brings a stick as a gift and wags tail furiously

triples.subject: German Shepherd
triples.object: Alert and noble, German shepherds patrol the campus perimeter with quiet confidence. They notice everything and trust slowly, but once bonded they are fiercely loyal.

triples.subject: German Shepherd
triples.object: breeds in spring, 63-day gestation, 4-8 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Husky
triples.object: Dramatic, talkative, and full of opinions, huskies howl at the moon, argue with the wind, and zoom across campus at full speed for absolutely no reason.

triples.subject: Husky
triples.object: breeds in winter, 63-day gestation, 4-6 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Corgi
triples.object: Short legs, enormous personality. Corgis rule the campus with disproportionate confidence and an unshakeable belief that everyone wants to see their butt wiggle.

triples.subject: Corgi
triples.object: breeds in spring, autumn, 63-day gestation, 3-5 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Shiba Inu
triples.object: Much campus. Very independence. Shiba inus act more like cats than dogs, doing exactly what they want and judging you silently from a distance. Wow.

triples.subject: Shiba Inu
triples.object: breeds in spring, 63-day gestation, 2-4 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Labrador
triples.object: Labs live for two things: food and love, in that order. They follow students around campus hoping for snacks and offer unconditional emotional support in return.

triples.subject: Labrador
triples.object: breeds in spring, autumn, 63-day gestation, 5-10 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Labrador
triples.object: enthusiastic tail wagging and bringing random objects

triples.subject: Border Collie
triples.object: The smartest animal on campus and they know it. Border collies solve puzzles, herd the other animals, and stare at you with unnerving intensity until you throw something.

triples.subject: Border Collie
triples.object: breeds in spring, 63-day gestation, 4-8 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Border Collie
triples.object: herds their partner into a corner and stares

triples.subject: Poodle
triples.object: breeds in spring, 63-day gestation, 3-6 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Serval
triples.object: The serval is an African wildcat built like a supermodel crossed with a satellite dish. Standing on the longest legs relative to body size of any cat, servals use their enormous cupped ears to pinpoin

triples.subject: Serval
triples.object: wanderer, predator, shy, solitary, territorial, crepuscular

triples.subject: Serval
triples.object: breeds in spring, 73-day gestation, 1-3 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Serval
triples.object: high-pitched chirping and purring calls that carry across the meadow at dusk, sometimes mistaken for a bird

triples.subject: Bengal Cat
triples.object: Wild-looking domestic cats with leopard rosettes and the energy of a small tornado. Bengals climb everything, splash in water features, and chatter at birds with murderous intent.

triples.subject: Bengal Cat
triples.object: breeds in spring, autumn, 63-day gestation, 2-4 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Bengal Cat
triples.object: intense staring contest followed by aggressive head bonks

triples.subject: Tiger
triples.object: The apex predator of the campus, somehow. Tigers pad through the forest on silent paws, radiating an aura of majestic danger. Students give them a wide berth but can\

triples.subject: Tiger
triples.object: breeds in winter, 105-day gestation, 1-3 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Tiger
triples.object: deep resonant roars that shake the leaves

triples.subject: Will-o\
triples.object: Faint bobbing lights that appear in the forest after dark. They drift between the trees with hypnotic rhythm, always staying just out of reach.

triples.subject: Moss Sprite
triples.object: Tiny plant-animal hybrids that look like walking clumps of moss. They tend the forest floor, nudging fallen leaves into tidy piles that no one asked for.

triples.subject: Moss Sprite
triples.object: breeds in spring, 60-day gestation, 1-3 offspring, asexual

triples.subject: Kitsune
triples.object: A fox spirit from Japanese folklore. It appears as an ordinary fox with an extra bushy tail, but those who watch carefully notice it moves with uncanny intelligence. Said to bring good fortune to thos

triples.subject: Kitsune
triples.object: breeds in autumn, 365-day gestation, 1-1 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Kitsune
triples.object: the pair dances around a circle of foxfire, tails intertwined

triples.subject: Glimmer Slug
triples.object: Nobody knows where they came from. They leave faintly luminescent trails that fade by morning. Some students swear the trails spell out words, but no two people read the same message.

triples.subject: Glimmer Slug
triples.object: breeds in autumn, 45-day gestation, 1-2 offspring, asexual

triples.subject: Glimmer Slug
triples.object: the slug splits luminescent fragments from its trail that grow into new slugs

triples.subject: Crow
triples.object: Wickedly intelligent birds that remember faces and hold grudges. They gather in noisy parliaments at dusk, debating crow politics no human will ever understand.

triples.subject: Crow
triples.object: flyer, scavenger, bold, herding, arboreal, diurnal

triples.subject: Crow
triples.object: breeds in spring, 18-day gestation, 3-6 offspring, monogamous

triples.subject: Crow
triples.object: mutual preening and gift-giving of shiny objects

triples.subject: Firefly
triples.object: Tiny lanterns that paint the summer darkness with morse code love letters. Each flash pattern is unique to the species — their light is the oldest dating app.

triples.subject: Firefly
triples.object: breeds in summer, 30-day gestation, 4-10 offspring, solitary

triples.subject: Capybara
triples.object: The original campus residents. These serene, chubby rodents lounge near the spa pond, playing leisurely games of tag and soaking in the warm water. Every student eventually befriends one.

triples.subject: Capybara
triples.object: wanderer, grazer, playful, communal, crepuscular, amphibious, docile

triples.subject: Capybara
triples.object: breeds in spring, autumn, 150-day gestation, 2-6 offspring, polygamous

triples.subject: Capybara
triples.object: males chase and vocalize with clicking sounds near the water
